description_long: This gatehouse features a robust arched stone entryway, constructed from large, irregularly shaped dark stones forming a heavy arch above a solid wooden door. The door is vertically planked, reinforced with black iron hinges and hardware, and set within a thick, deeply recessed stone frame. The stonework is weathered, with visible textural variation and chiseled details, giving the structure a medieval and fortified appearance. The sides of the gatehouse are solid stone pillars, with no visible windows or decorative elements, emphasizing its defensive function. The overall structure is squat and wide, with a pronounced keystone at the apex of the arch and substantial stone bases at either side. The rear and side views reveal the depth and massiveness of the stonework, with the arch and door as the only points of entry.
description: A fortified stone gatehouse with a heavy arched entry and a reinforced wooden door, exuding a medieval, defensive character.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched stone entryway and the full wooden door with iron hardware.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thick stone construction and the depth of the archway, with no visible openings.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, highlighting the arch and door but with less visible detail on the door hardware.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the solid stone pillar and the depth of the structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone doorway with a vertically planked wooden door and iron hardware.
attribute_window_placement: No windows visible on any side.
